records.query: Why would you keep every change as a permanent record instead of just updating a row in a table?
records.hw3_comment: The flagship hybrid win, on exactly the query the filter cannot help (inference correctly returns none — the vocabulary is ambiguous). BM25 promoted Event Sourcing Basics ('never deleted. A correction is expressed as a further event, in the way a ledger is corrected by a reversing entry') and Projections And Read Models from semantic ranks 4–5 into the top-2 — both better answers than the baseline's top-1. The three-document leak shrinks to one foreign chunk at rank 3; top-3 precision goes from 1/3 to 2/3.

Without the filter this query would have LOST precision to hybrid.

records.query: What has to be true for the platform to handle 5,000 requests per second?
records.hw3_comment: The honest negative example. BM25 promoted the Observability chunk to top-1 over the Load Profile chunk that HW2 judged the best answer — the breadcrumb repeats '5,000 Requests per Second' in every chunk of this document, so the lexical branch amplifies exactly the title-token inflation HW2 flagged on the semantic side. Still the right document, still a hit, and the displaced chunk sits at rank 2, but qualitatively the top-1 got slightly worse. This is the design doc's breadcrumb-bias limitation showing up on schedule.

records.query: Who confirms that a delivery actually happened, and what does that confirmation trigger?
records.hw3_comment: The flagship filter win. Baseline rank 2 was a CQRS chunk that drifted in on the word 'event'; the concept-guide filter (inferred from 'delivery'/'confirmation') evicts it, and the improved top-3 is all-primer — including The Load Lifecycle chunk that BM25 surfaces at lexical rank 1. Top-3 precision goes from 2/3 to 3/3. Crucially, hybrid-only made this query WORSE (the CQRS chunk reached top-1), so the gain here belongs to filtering and to filtering constraining hybrid — the clearest single demonstration of why the combined pipeline needs both parts.

records.query: What is the best way to fine-tune a large language model on a custom dataset?
records.hw3_comment: Correctly unfiltered — no inference rule matches, which is the designed behaviour: a filter would have funnelled an unanswerable question into one document and made the results look more authoritative than they are. Hybrid reshuffled which irrelevant chunks appear (BM25 latches onto 'model'-adjacent migration vocabulary), but the semantic ceiling is unchanged at 0.266, still well below the 0.413 in-corpus floor. The HW2 conclusion stands: only a score floor, out of scope here as in HW2, would let the system decline to answer.
